justification: Bird correctly identifies the question as out-of-scope and escalates appropriately, but still populates the full domain_analysis structure (domain_analysis, business_rules, acceptance_criteria, edge_cases, business_impact, confidence) with empty/N/A values rather than deflecting briefly. The rubric requires no acceptance_criteria, business_rules, or invariants produced and a concise response of a few sentences at most. While the fields are empty arrays, the structured output format itself implies a domain analysis was performed. The escalation content is correct and well-reasoned, but the response is not the brief deflection the rubric expects.

observations.text: Correctly identifies the question as pure CS theory with no domain content
Escalation clearly explains why this is out of scope and recommends redirecting
No actual business rules, acceptance criteria, or invariants were fabricated
Still populates the full structured output template (domain_analysis, confidence, business_impact) rather than responding with a brief deflection
confidence.level is set to 99, which the fail rubric flags as problematic — a confidence score was assigned for a domain analysis that should not have been attempted
Response is not concise — full JSON structure with many fields instead of a few sentences

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"The request asks for a general computer science explanation of binary search tree insertion time complexity and red-black tree balancing mechanisms.
